expect: PHIL-5a DROP criterion is rewritten from impossible net-loss to measurable accessible-knowledge decline using MUST-KNOW+ACTIVE from knowledge_state; frontier/challenge text reflects the new criterion and current S537 baseline.
actual: CONFIRMED. knowledge_state.py exposes the accessibility states needed for PHIL-5a: MUST-KNOW=481, ACTIVE=524, DECAYED=519, BLIND-SPOT=160 at S537. PHIL-5a was rewritten from raw net-file-loss to an accessibility balance criterion: DROP if DECAYED+BLIND-SPOT exceeds MUST-KNOW+ACTIVE for 20 consecutive sessions. beliefs/PHILOSOPHY.md and F-EPIS3 now use the measurable criterion.
diff: Expected an accessibility-based rewrite and got one. The final criterion is stronger than simple raw decline because accessible-vs-inaccessible balance is less confounded by compaction and cleanup than file-count deltas.
criterion_before: DROP if net knowledge loss (supersession - creation) >0 sustained over 50 sessions
criterion_after: DROP if DECAYED+BLIND-SPOT exceeds MUST-KNOW+ACTIVE for 20 consecutive sessions in knowledge_state.py snapshots
